subtasks.name: Define Membership Tiers
subtasks.description: Develop clear, tiered membership levels for the 'Clay Pass' with distinct benefits (e.g., basic access, priority booking, exclusive events) based on resident feedback and budget constraints. Include pricing structures aligned with local income levels and ensure all tiers are accessible to diverse community members.

subtasks.name: Validate Pricing Strategy
subtasks.description: Conduct focus groups with local residents to test proposed pricing tiers and benefits, gather feedback on perceived value, and refine the offering before launch. Use real-time survey data to adjust tiers and messaging to maximize uptake and inclusivity.

subtasks.name: Design Onboarding Experience
subtasks.description: Create a welcoming, multilingual onboarding process for new 'Clay Pass' members, including digital sign-up, orientation video, welcome kit, and access to introductory workshops. Ensure materials are available in both Danish and Greenlandic and reflect cultural sensitivity.

subtasks.name: Integrate with Booking System
subtasks.description: Implement the 'Clay Pass' membership tiers into the online booking platform with automated access controls, tier-specific privileges, and real-time availability tracking. Conduct usability testing with diverse users to ensure clarity and accessibility.

subtasks.name: Launch Soft-Tiered Trial Version
subtasks.description: Roll out a pilot version of the 'Clay Pass' with flexible access and limited benefits to test demand, collect user feedback, and identify operational bottlenecks. Monitor participation rates and adjust the model based on early insights before full launch.

Data collection:
## 1. Arctic Shipping Risk Assessment

The plan assumes reliable quarterly shipments from Denmark, but without historical data on Arctic weather impacts, this assumption is unvalidated. A failure to account for seasonal shipping risks could lead to session cancellations, reputational damage, and financial collapse.

### Data to Collect

- Historical shipping delay data for Nuuk Harbour (2018–2025)
- Ice blockage frequency and duration in Denmark Strait during winter months
- Average customs clearance time at Nuuk port
- Probability of >4-week shipment delays due to storms or ice
- Real-time Arctic shipping alert integration capability

### Simulation Steps

- Use Danish Maritime Authority (DMA) API to extract historical shipping logs from 2018–2025
- Run a risk modeling simulation in Python using Pandas and Matplotlib to map delay patterns by month
- Validate model output against Greenlandic Port Authority public reports
- Simulate emergency air freight response time using Nordic Cargo’s transit data (72-hour max)

### Expert Validation Steps

- Engage a Supply Chain Analyst specializing in Arctic logistics to review the risk model
- Submit findings to the Danish Maritime Authority for cross-verification
- Present results to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for cultural context on shipping disruptions

### Responsible Parties

- Supply Chain Coordinator
- Project Manager
- Financial Analyst

### Assumptions

- **High:** Quarterly clay shipments from Denmark will be on time with no major delays.
- **Medium:** Emergency air freight can be activated within 72 hours of detection.

### SMART Validation Objective

By March 31, 2026, produce a validated Arctic shipping risk assessment report showing a 90% confidence interval for winter delay probabilities, with recommendations for biannual import scheduling aligned with navigation windows.

### Notes

- This validation must precede any finalization of the supply chain contract.
- Delay in this step jeopardizes the entire Year 1 launch timeline.


## 2. Winter Thermal Load Simulation

Passive solar and modular kilns are assumed sufficient, but without real-world validation under extreme winter conditions, the system may fail—leading to frozen clay, mold, or unsafe operations. This directly threatens safety, compliance, and project viability.

### Data to Collect

- Temperature gradients across drying zones under 90% occupancy
- Humidity levels during peak kiln operation
- CO₂ buildup in enclosed spaces
- Energy consumption during simulated 4-hour session
- Thermal stability of passive solar design at -20°C outdoor temps

### Simulation Steps

- Deploy IoT sensors (temperature, humidity, CO₂) in a mock workshop setup in December 2025
- Simulate 14 people + 70% kiln load for 4 hours using temporary heating systems
- Run dynamic thermal modeling in EnergyPlus software with real-world boundary conditions
- Compare simulation outputs with physical sensor readings using statistical regression analysis

### Expert Validation Steps

- Engage an Arctic-certified building engineer to audit the EnergyPlus model and sign off on thermal readiness
- Submit thermal stress test results to the National Labour Inspectorate (Greenland) for compliance review
- Present findings to the Facility Manager and Safety Officer for operational planning

### Responsible Parties

- Facility Manager
- Safety Officer
- Energy Systems Engineer

### Assumptions

- **High:** Passive solar design and thermal mass walls can maintain stable indoor conditions during peak winter use.
- **Medium:** The 'thermal curfew' protocol is sufficient to prevent system overload.

### SMART Validation Objective

By July 15, 2026, complete a full-scale thermal stress test and deliver a signed Winter Thermal Readiness Report confirming that all zones remain within safe operating parameters (±5°C variance) under 90% winter occupancy.

### Notes

- Testing must occur before fit-out completion to allow for insulation upgrades.
- Failure to validate risks regulatory shutdown by Nuuk Municipality.


## 3. Community Intellectual Property Rights (CIPR) Framework

Without a formal CIPR framework, the project risks cultural appropriation, legal action, and community boycotts. The 'Inuit Clay Legacy Project' cannot proceed ethically without sovereign community control over sacred narratives.

### Data to Collect

- List of culturally sensitive motifs and stories restricted from public display
- Tiered access rules for exhibitions and digital content
- Approval process for elder-led representation of traditions
- Consent documentation templates for narrative use
- Guidelines for co-creation with elders and artisans

### Simulation Steps

- Conduct a virtual workshop with elders and artists using Zoom to draft CIPR principles
- Create a digital consent form template in both Danish and Greenlandic using Notion
- Map out a decision-making hierarchy for cultural content approval via flowchart
- Simulate a sample exhibition proposal through the CIPR review process

### Expert Validation Steps

- Engage an Indigenous research ethics consultant from the Inuit Circumpolar Council to audit the framework
- Present the draft CIPR document to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for formal endorsement
- Obtain legal review from the National Labour Inspectorate (Greenland) on compliance with labor and IP laws

### Responsible Parties

- Cultural Liaison
- Project Manager
- Legal Advisor

### Assumptions

- **High:** Elders and local artists will provide timely consent and guidance on cultural content.
- **High:** Digital archives and AI tools can be used safely with community consent.

### SMART Validation Objective

By March 31, 2026, finalize and obtain formal approval of a CIPR framework from the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council, including a signed agreement and a list of approved/forbidden motifs.

### Notes

- This validation is a prerequisite for launching any public-facing cultural program.
- Delaying this step invalidates the entire Cultural Anchoring Framework.


## 4. Material Co-Creation Working Group Trials

Using untested local materials risks producing unusable ceramics, wasting resources, and undermining cultural authenticity. Without technical and cultural validation, the Material Adaptation Strategy is speculative and potentially harmful.

### Data to Collect

- Shrinkage rate of blended clay (imported + glacial sand/volcanic ash)
- Firing temperature stability and cracking incidence
- Kiln compatibility of locally sourced minerals
- Elder and artisan feedback on material authenticity
- Scientific and oral history documentation of trial results

### Simulation Steps

- Prepare 10 small batches of blended clay (varying ratios: 70% imported / 30% local)
- Fire samples at standard temperatures (1000°C–1200°C) using modular kilns
- Measure shrinkage, weight loss, and structural integrity post-firing
- Record observations in bilingual field notebooks (Danish/Greenlandic)
- Host a joint review session with elders and artisans to interpret results

### Expert Validation Steps

- Engage a Greenlandic Materials Scientist to analyze mineral composition and firing behavior
- Submit trial data to the University of Greenland for peer review
- Present findings to the Material Co-Creation Working Group for consensus approval

### Responsible Parties

- Cultural Liaison
- Facility Manager
- Materials Scientist

### Assumptions

- **High:** Locally sourced glacial sand or volcanic ash is compatible with standard kiln processes.
- **High:** Elders will approve the use of specific materials after testing.

### SMART Validation Objective

By May 1, 2026, complete three successful trial batches with unanimous approval from the Material Co-Creation Working Group and publish a joint technical-oral report.

### Notes

- No new materials may be used until trials are completed and approved.
- This validation is mandatory before any material sourcing begins.


## 5. Volunteer Instructor Program Redesign

Treating volunteers as backup staff creates legal and safety risks. Without formal training, liability coverage, and integration, a single incident could result in closure. The program must be restructured as a professional fellowship.

### Data to Collect

- Training curriculum for safety and cultural protocols
- Liability waiver and insurance coverage documentation
- Certification pathway for junior instructors
- Performance tracking and evaluation metrics
- Onboarding kit in Danish and Greenlandic

### Simulation Steps

- Develop a 12-hour certification program using Canva and Google Forms
- Simulate a volunteer training session via Zoom with role-playing scenarios
- Generate digital badges using badge.io platform
- Test the onboarding kit with retired artists in a dry-run session
- Integrate performance tracking into a shared dashboard (Notion)

### Expert Validation Steps

- Engage a Volunteer Management Specialist to review the program structure
- Submit the revised program to the National Labour Inspectorate for compliance check
- Present the final design to the Advisory Council for cultural alignment

### Responsible Parties

- Safety Officer
- Cultural Liaison
- Human Resources Lead

### Assumptions

- **High:** Retired artists can be trained and integrated safely into teaching roles.
- **Medium:** A $500,000 general liability policy is available and affordable.

### SMART Validation Objective

By April 15, 2026, launch a fully operational 'Community Teaching Fellowship' with 100% certified participants, signed waivers, and active insurance coverage.

### Notes

- The original volunteer pool must be replaced entirely.
- This validation is required before any instructor-led sessions begin.

## Summary

The most critical next steps are validating Arctic shipping risks, winter thermal loads, cultural IP rights, material feasibility, and volunteer program redesign. These five areas contain high-sensitivity assumptions that, if invalidated, would compromise the entire project's viability. Immediate action must focus on commissioning expert reviews, conducting physical simulations, and securing formal community approvals—all before any final contracts or construction milestones are locked in. Failure to validate these assumptions risks operational failure, legal liability, and loss of community trust.

schedule quarterly coordination meetings at least two months in advance; co-develop exhibition themes and timelines in writing with mutual sign-off to ensure alignment and reduce last-minute revisions.","days_min":14,"days_max":45,"days_realistic":30}},{"id":"466d26da-b322-4e72-9a1f-88a6b6065e76","description":"Integrate Traditional Forms and Narratives into Beginner Courses","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays may occur if elders or artists are unavailable for consultation, if there is misalignment between traditional content and beginner-level pedagogy, or if cultural sensitivities lead to revisions after initial drafts.","mitigation_strategy":"Engage elders and artists through formal advisory council meetings with scheduled input windows; develop draft course modules for review with the council before finalization; incorporate feedback loops with clear decision-making protocols to avoid prolonged revision cycles.","days_min":21,"days_max":60,"days_realistic":40}},{"id":"9662c55b-6978-490d-8697-27b628054d06","description":"Ensure All Communications and Signage Are Bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic)","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays may arise from translation inaccuracies, lack of qualified bilingual staff, or approval bottlenecks from community reviewers, especially if cultural nuance is not properly preserved.","mitigation_strategy":"Partner with a certified Greenlandic language specialist and a local cultural liaison to oversee all translations; risk of non-compliance with environmental or safety standards if sourcing is not properly vetted.","mitigation_strategy":"Pre-approve all material suppliers through the advisory council and compliance team; unexpected shifts in tourism trends or local participation rates could lead to underutilization or overcrowding.","mitigation_strategy":"Leverage initial soft-launch data and real-time tracking dashboards to refine forecasts iteratively; conduct monthly review cycles with stakeholder input to adjust programming based on actual utilization and feedback.","days_min":15,"days_max":30,"days_realistic":20}},{"id":"b1128f26-2b64-4b11-bf16-95ec7aedc381","description":"Evaluate Attendance and Utilization Metrics Monthly","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delayed or inconsistent data collection from sensors and user feedback systems may hinder accurate performance evaluation; low response rates from community surveys could skew results.","mitigation_strategy":"Automate data collection via IoT sensors and digital dashboards to ensure continuous monitoring; incentivize survey participation with small community rewards and distribute bilingual forms through trusted local networks to boost engagement.","days_min":10,"days_max":25,"days_realistic":18}}]},{"id":"8730af61-7cdf-4844-88c0-d746ef7d41c0","description":"Launch Hybrid Access & Revenue Architecture","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays in securing community buy-in or participation due to cultural sensitivities, misaligned expectations, or low turnout for co-creation events. accessibility.","days_min":10,"days_max":30,"days_realistic":20}},{"id":"8a4b9e20-bc97-4ad8-9ebd-054b2d20561a","description":"Monitor Revenue Predictability and Adjust Models alternatives.","days_min":14,"days_max":28,"days_realistic":21}},{"id":"8335a69d-118f-47ed-a1a2-841cefa523f0","description":"Launch Youth Apprenticeship Track with School guidance.","days_min":28,"days_max":56,"days_realistic":42}},{"id":"1379669b-9bd3-4ec5-b102-e91d9f11be38","description":"Introduce AI-Assisted Design Tools with Cultural Context wary of technology replacing handcraft. Delays may also arise if survey tools are not available in both Danish and Greenlandic or if translation errors affect comprehension.","mitigation_strategy":"Distribute surveys via multiple channels (in-person at events, printed materials, SMS, and bilingual digital forms); offer small incentives (e.g., free workshop vouchers) for participation; partner with local schools and youth groups to promote engagement; use pre-tested, culturally appropriate language and visuals; conduct bi-monthly review meetings to demonstrate how feedback leads to real changes.","days_min":7,"days_max":21,"days_realistic":14}},{"id":"6eb014ec-ba1e-41ae-aa39-79803a16ef41","description":"Publish Co-Created Artworks in Public Spaces and Digital Platforms","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays may result from incomplete artwork approvals due to unresolved cultural sensitivities, technical issues with digital publishing platforms, or delays in printing and installation logistics\u2014especially during winter months when transport is unreliable.","mitigation_strategy":"Establish a clear approval workflow involving the advisory council before finalizing artworks; create a digital archive with version control and metadata; schedule physical installations during spring/summer months when transport is more reliable; maintain backup printing contracts with local providers and use weather-resilient mounting solutions for outdoor displays.","days_min":10,"days_max":35,"days_realistic":21}}]},{"id":"07827060-b275-4773-a9ac-629b6f5ff467","description":"Ensure Regulatory Compliance and Stakeholder Engagement","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays in securing final approvals from Nuuk Municipality or unexpected regulatory feedback could push back the completion of pre-opening milestones. Additionally, last-minute changes in lease terms or facility inspection results may require rework.","mitigation_strategy":"Begin all permit and compliance applications early with bilingual documentation; maintain a buffer of at least two weeks in the timeline for review cycles; assign a dedicated liaison to coordinate with Nuuk Municipality and ensure timely responses.","days_min":14,"days_max":35,"days_realistic":21},"task_children":[{"id":"2e6f29b3-d7ad-47a9-9de1-f540013231eb","description":"Submit All Permit Applications in Danish and Greenlandic by April 20, 2026","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"The certified Arctic building specialist may be unavailable due to high demand during winter months, or unforeseen structural issues discovered during the audit could require redesigns. Delays in scheduling or access to the site would also impact progress.","mitigation_strategy":"Book the certified Arctic specialist at least six weeks in advance; conduct preliminary site assessments before the audit to identify potential red flags; maintain a contingency plan with a secondary qualified auditor on standby.","days_min":10,"days_max":25,"days_realistic":18}},{"id":"de656ef5-e6de-4587-94ec-871e9d4df59b","description":"Conduct Pre-Opening Thermal and Safety Audits with Certified Arctic Specialist","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"The Building Code Compliance Certificate or Fire Safety Certification may be delayed due to incomplete documentation, missing inspections, or non-compliance with Arctic-specific safety standards. Equipment or design flaws discovered late could require costly revisions.","mitigation_strategy":"Prepare all required documentation in both Danish and Greenlandic well in advance; conduct internal mock inspections using checklists aligned with NFPA and Bygningsreglementet; engage the certification bodies early for pre-submission feedback to avoid rejections.","days_min":12,"days_max":30,"days_realistic":20}},{"id":"e064f302-6662-409b-ab28-ea8b2deaddfa","description":"Obtain Building Code Compliance Certificate and Fire Safety Certification","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays in obtaining the Building Code Compliance Certificate and Fire Safety Certification may occur due to incomplete documentation, missing inspections, or regulatory backlogs from Nuuk Municipality, especially during peak review periods.","mitigation_strategy":"Submit all required documentation in both Danish and Greenlandic well in advance (by April 20, 2026), conduct a pre-submission review with the Katuaq Cultural Centre Oversight Committee, and schedule follow-up meetings with Nuuk Municipality planning officials to track progress and resolve issues proactively.","days_min":10,"days_max":30,"days_realistic":20}},{"id":"c5c72c04-1b52-4cc1-a006-f0602d2e1bdb","description":"Schedule Compliance Audit with Nuuk Municipality One Week Before Opening","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Incomplete or inaccurate records of safety training, liability waivers, and incident logs could delay compliance verification, particularly if staff fail to submit documentation on time or if language barriers hinder recordkeeping.","mitigation_strategy":"Implement a centralized digital tracking system with automated reminders for staff to log training and waiver completion; conduct bi-weekly audits of records and provide bilingual support for documentation; assign the Safety Officer to oversee compliance and report directly to the Clay Workshop Manager.","days_min":7,"days_max":21,"days_realistic":14}},{"id":"eb18f71b-4b10-4961-9177-7077eb30acc5","description":"Maintain Records of Safety Training, Liability Waivers, and Incident Logs","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Failure to maintain up-to-date records of safety training, liability waivers, and incident logs\u2014due to staff turnover, poor coordination, or lack of accountability\u2014could result in non-compliance during the final audit, leading to delays in opening.","mitigation_strategy":"Integrate record maintenance into onboarding and monthly performance reviews; use the Digital Shift Calendar to flag upcoming training deadlines; designate a rotating compliance champion among instructors to ensure consistent oversight and timely submissions.","days_min":5,"days_max":15,"days_realistic":10}}]},{"id":"1749a87b-16d7-40fa-8683-e8d69b60fc6f","description":"Execute Pre-Opening Milestones and Final Readiness Checks","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays in securing final approvals from Nuuk Municipality, unexpected issues during fit-out requiring rework, or last-minute changes in staffing or equipment delivery could push back the pre-opening milestones. Additionally, unforeseen regulatory hurdles or compliance gaps may require additional time to resolve.","mitigation_strategy":"Proactively coordinate with Nuuk Municipality and regulatory bodies through scheduled check-ins; conduct a dry run of all compliance documentation prior to submission; maintain a buffer of 10 days in the timeline for unexpected delays; and assign a dedicated compliance liaison to monitor progress and escalate issues early.","days_min":15,"days_max":45,"days_realistic":30},"task_children":[{"id":"d5f4143f-516a-4811-8204-5dcfad0f8432","description":"Secure Lease Agreement for 120\u2013150 m\u00b2 Space Near Katuaq","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Lease negotiations may stall due to landlord availability, legal review delays, or disagreements over terms such as rent escalation clauses or use restrictions. Winter conditions could also delay site access or inspections needed for lease finalization.","mitigation_strategy":"Engage a local real estate advisor familiar with Nuuk commercial leases to expedite negotiations; prepare a standardized lease agreement draft in both Danish and Greenlandic for faster review; schedule site visits and legal reviews in parallel; and include a contingency clause allowing for temporary space use if needed while finalizing the primary lease.","days_min":10,"days_max":35,"days_realistic":25}},{"id":"de3088ee-cfeb-4f03-bd04-25d2f8dd1511","description":"Hire and Onboard Four Part-Time Instructors","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Instructor hiring may be delayed due to limited local talent pool, lack of qualified applicants, or extended onboarding timelines. Language barriers or cultural integration challenges could also slow down effective team integration.","mitigation_strategy":"Launch a targeted recruitment campaign in collaboration with local schools, Katuaq Cultural Centre, and tourism networks; offer competitive part-time compensation and flexible scheduling; implement a structured onboarding program with bilingual training materials and peer mentorship; and begin hiring 4 weeks before the target start date to allow for overlap and adjustment.","days_min":14,"days_max":42,"days_realistic":28}},{"id":"cdf0bac9-9c4f-4332-8560-6438d65da866","description":"Complete Fit-Out and Install Kilns, Worktables, Drying Racks, and Ventilation","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Delays in delivery or installation of kilns, worktables, drying racks, and ventilation systems due to Arctic shipping disruptions, supplier backlogs, or unforeseen logistical challenges during winter months. Additionally, potential for design changes or safety compliance issues requiring rework.","mitigation_strategy":"Pre-order all major equipment with guaranteed delivery timelines; maintain a buffer of 10 days in the schedule; conduct pre-installation inspections with certified Arctic contractors; prioritize modular, pre-fabricated components to reduce on-site assembly time.","days_min":21,"days_max":42,"days_realistic":30}},{"id":"77392e7a-c4f2-4f3c-bb45-4846b1ad175f","description":"Deliver First Course to Soft Launch Audience on August 1, 2026","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"First course delivery may be delayed due to last-minute instructor availability, incomplete training, or unresolved supply chain issues (e.g., clay shortages). Weather-related access problems to the workshop could also impact the soft launch date.","mitigation_strategy":"Implement a cross-trained instructor rotation with digital shift calendar tracking; ensure all instructors complete mandatory safety and curriculum training at least two weeks prior; confirm clay buffer stock is on-site and verified before launch; conduct a dry-run session one week before August 1 to test logistics and staffing coverage.","days_min":7,"days_max":14,"days_realistic":10}},{"id":"e6ac1ce4-e57f-4687-8fb4-47772bf6e944","description":"Achieve 100% Session Coverage with No Cancellations Due to Supply or Staffing Issues","extra_fields":{"delay_risks":"Session cancellations may occur due to unexpected staff absences (illness, travel delays), material shortages despite buffer stock, or last-minute equipment failures not caught during fit-out. Volunteer liability issues could also lead to operational halts if waivers are incomplete.","mitigation_strategy":"Enforce a 100% completion of liability waivers and safety training for all staff and volunteers before any session; implement real-time monitoring via IoT sensors and digital dashboard to preempt supply or environmental issues; maintain a rotating backup instructor roster and use the digital shift calendar to auto-reassign sessions in case of absence.","days_min":14,"days_max":28,"days_realistic":21}}]}]}}
